title: Cloud Computing
description: Essential CLI commands and configurations for cloud computing tasks.

commands.description: List all S3 buckets in your AWS account
commands.scenario: Use when you need to view all S3 buckets available in your AWS account.

commands.description: List all Google Cloud Compute Engine instances
commands.scenario: Use to get a list of all VM instances running in Google Cloud.

commands.description: List all Azure virtual machines in table format
commands.scenario: Use this to quickly see all VMs in your Azure account in a readable format.
Run this to prepare your Terraform directory for other commands.

commands.description: List all pods in the current Kubernetes namespace
commands.scenario: Use to check the status of all pods in your Kubernetes cluster.

commands.description: Describe one or more EC2 instances
commands.scenario: Use to get detailed information about your EC2 instances.

commands.description: Authenticate gcloud CLI with your Google account
commands.scenario: Run this to log in and authenticate your Google Cloud CLI session.

commands.description: Authenticate Azure CLI with your Azure account
commands.scenario: Use this command to log in to your Azure account via CLI.

commands.description: Apply the changes required to reach the desired state of the configuration
commands.scenario: Run this to apply your Terraform configuration to your cloud infrastructure.

commands.description: Apply a Kubernetes configuration from a file
commands.scenario: Use to deploy or update resources in a Kubernetes cluster using a YAML file.

commands.description: Configure AWS CLI with your credentials
commands.scenario: Run this to set up your AWS CLI with access keys and region.

commands.description: Set the active Google Cloud project
commands.scenario: Use to switch the active project in your gcloud CLI session.

commands.description: Create a new Azure resource group
commands.scenario: Use this to organize your Azure resources into a new group.

commands.description: Generate and show an execution plan
commands.scenario: Run this to see what actions Terraform will take to change infrastructure.

commands.description: Delete a specific pod in Kubernetes
commands.scenario: Use to remove a malfunctioning or unneeded pod from your cluster.
